title: Spanakopita
subtitle: Greek spinach and feta pie in buttered filo pastry.
excerpt: Spinach and feta filling seasoned with dill and nutmeg, layered between sheets of butter-brushed filo pastry and baked until shattering and golden. The best Greek pie and one of the best things you can do with feta. Serves six.
sourceNotes: Spanakopita. The dish is one of the foundational pites (pies) of Greek cooking, documented throughout Greek regional cookbooks. Claudia Roden's The Food of the Eastern Mediterranean and Diana Henry's A Bird in the Hand both document versions. The filo technique follows standard Greek practice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked, well-wrapped, for up to one month. Bake from frozen, adding 10 minutes to the cooking time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble the day before and refrigerate unbaked. Brush with butter just before baking.

content.text: The main enemies of spanakopita are moisture and rushed filo. Spinach releases a large amount of water; if it is not squeezed properly, the filling will be wet and the filo underneath will turn to paste. Each filo sheet must be brushed with butter before the next goes on; stacked dry sheets seal to each other without crisping properly.

items.prepNote: frozen, defrosted and squeezed very dry; or 800 g fresh, wilted and squeezed

content.text: If using frozen spinach, defrost and squeeze in a clean tea towel until completely dry. Cook {{onion}} diced onion in {{olive-oil}} of olive oil over medium heat for 8 minutes until soft. Add {{garlic}} of grated garlic; cook 1 minute. Leave to cool. Combine the spinach, cooled onion mixture, {{feta}} of crumbled feta, {{eggs}} beaten eggs, {{dill}} of dill, {{nutmeg-ground}} of nutmeg and {{black-pepper}} of pepper. Taste before adding salt; feta is already salty.

Heat the oven to 190°C. Brush a 20 x 30 cm baking tin with {{unsalted-butter}} of melted butter. Lay one sheet of {{filo-pastry}} filo in the tin, letting it overhang the edges. Brush with butter. Repeat with 5 more sheets, brushing each one. Spread the spinach filling evenly over the pastry. Fold the overhanging edges over the filling. Layer the remaining filo sheets on top, brushing each with butter. Tuck in the edges and brush the top generously.

Score the top layers of filo into portions with a sharp knife before baking; this prevents the pastry shattering when cut after cooking. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes until deep golden and the filling is set. Leave to cool for 10 minutes before cutting.

items.fix: Squeeze frozen spinach in a tea towel until no more water comes out. This is the most important step in the recipe.

items.cause: Dried out while working with it.
items.fix: Keep unused filo sheets covered with a damp tea towel while you work; they dry out very quickly.

items.cause: Spinach diluted the feta, or under-seasoned.
items.fix: Feta should dominate the filling. Taste before baking; if it is flat, add a small amount of salt and more black pepper.

content.text: Spanakopita is one of the most enduring things in Greek cooking: a pie made from the simplest ingredients that travels well, feeds a crowd and tastes as good at room temperature as it does hot. It belongs on a table with other mezze, or as a main dish on a Friday when there is nothing in the fridge except feta, spinach and filo.
